desc: Tyre is a South Lebanon, Mediterranean coast base shaped by unesco phoenician and roman ruins, south lebanon beaches, old harbour lanes. It suits archaeology plus beach trips and rewards timing around the local weather curve, because crowding, room rates and transfer friction shift noticeably by season.

todo.name: Al Mina archaeological site
todo.desc: Walk Roman streets and harbour-side ruins that reveal Tyre's long Mediterranean role.

todo.name: Al Bass archaeological site
todo.desc: Visit the necropolis, triumphal arch and hippodrome for the city's monumental Roman layer.

todo.name: Tyre Coast Nature Reserve
todo.desc: Use the protected beach area for dunes, nesting-season awareness and a less built-up coast.

todo.name: Old harbour dinner
todo.desc: Finish with seafood around the small harbour when conditions and opening hours line up.

wifi.note: Typical visitor connectivity in Tyre is usable around central stays, but backup mobile data is wise for day trips and smaller guesthouses.

prac.fltNote: Tyre has no airport; Beirut is the gateway and road travel south should be planned around current security advice and checkpoint realities.
prac.currency: Lebanese pound (LBP); US dollars are also widely used in some travel contexts
prac.lang: Arabic; French and English are common in Beirut and tourism services
prac.visa: UK/Irish passport holders should verify Lebanon visa-on-arrival and security-related entry rules before travel.
alerts: Check official travel advice for South Lebanon before planning.
Summer beach demand is local and weekend-heavy.
Use trusted drivers for Beirut transfers.

sweet: May, Jun and Oct usually give the best balance of warmth, open seasonal services and less pressure than the absolute midsummer peak.
